activities_projects.title: Work Done with Inclined Force
activities_projects.objective: Calculate work when force is applied at an angle on an inclined plane
steps: Place a block on an inclined plane
Apply force at a known angle to move the block
Measure distance along the incline
Calculate work using W = F × d × cosθ
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands effect of force direction and slope on work

activities_projects.title: Power Calculation
activities_projects.objective: Calculate power for a given work and time
steps: Lift or move a weight over a known distance in a measured time
Calculate work done
Calculate power using P = W/t
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects work and time to power

activities_projects.title: Efficiency of Combined Machines
activities_projects.objective: Calculate overall efficiency when using multiple simple machines
steps: Use a system of pulleys and lever to lift a weight
Measure input force and distance, output force and height
Calculate efficiency = (Output work/Input work) × 100%
Combine efficiency of both machines if used together
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands complex machine efficiency and energy loss

activities_projects.title: Work Against Friction with Inclined Plane
activities_projects.objective: Analyze work done to overcome friction on slopes
steps: Pull block along inclined rough plane with measured force
Measure distance moved along incline
Calculate work done against friction using W = F × d
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates friction, slope, and work in real-life scenarios

activities_projects.title: Mechanical Energy Conservation
activities_projects.objective: Verify conservation of total mechanical energy
steps: Release pendulum or car from a height
Measure speed at different points
Calculate PE + KE at multiple points
Verify that total mechanical energy remains approximately constant
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes real-world energy conservation with losses

activities_projects.title: Work and Power in Daily Tasks
activities_projects.objective: Compare work and power in different daily-life activities
steps: Select two daily tasks involving lifting or moving objects
Measure time and force for each task
Calculate work and power for both tasks
Compare efficiency and energy expenditure
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects physics concepts to real-life efficiency

activities_projects.title: Work Done by Variable Force
activities_projects.objective: Understand work done when force is not constant
steps: Move an object with varying force over a distance
Record force at intervals
Calculate approximate work done using W = Σ F × Δd
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies work formula for non-uniform forces

activities_projects.title: Kinetic Energy with Different Speeds
activities_projects.objective: Compare KE for different velocities and same mass
steps: Push toy car at different speeds
Measure speed and mass
Calculate kinetic energy using KE = 1/2 mv^2
Compare energy for different speeds
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes effect of velocity on KE

activities_projects.title: Potential Energy in Multi-Height System
activities_projects.objective: Analyze PE at different heights and masses
steps: Lift objects of different masses to different heights
Calculate potential energy for each object using PE = mgh
Compare PE values and analyze effect of mass and height
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Deepens understanding of potential energy dependencies

activities_projects.title: Energy Loss Due to Non-Ideal Conditions
activities_projects.objective: Measure energy lost to friction or air resistance
steps: Roll ball down ramp on smooth and rough surfaces
Measure final speed or height in both cases
Calculate mechanical energy lost due to friction or air resistance
Explain difference in energy
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands real-life energy dissipation and efficiency
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate measurements, calculations, and reasoning (10 marks)
